customer_anxiety_profile: Low anxiety, high motivation. Michael is self-qualified — he initiated contact, has two EVs driving up his load, is aware of Dominion rate increases, and has already done basic kWh math on his own bill. His primary concern is installation complexity on a slate roof; he has a strong preference for ground mount panels to avoid structural damage. He is not skeptical of solar or of Ion — his questions are logistical, not defensive.
unresolved_concerns: Slate roof installation — Michael explicitly raised concern about structural damage and expressed preference for ground mount panels in backyard and front yard. This was not addressed or bridged to the closer.

Appointment format — Michael asked twice whether the meeting would be in-person or a phone call. The rep gave inconsistent answers ('in person' then 'either that or they'll knock'). Michael may not have a clear picture of what he agreed to.
Wife not included — Michael's wife is on the title and was not included in the appointment. She is a co-decision-maker whose absence is a close risk.
sensitive_topics_raised: Rep identity inconsistency — rep introduced as 'Matthew with Niles' at 00:04 but texted Michael as 'Bucky with Ion' at 04:42. If Michael notices the discrepancy before the appointment, it may raise a legitimacy question. Closer should be prepared to address it if it comes up.
exact_promises_rep_made: Appointment booked for Friday the 20th at 10AM.
Rep will text Michael before the appointment.
Rep said 'I'll get working on your design' — implies a design will be ready or in progress for the appointment.
closer_should_know: Michael is a strong lead — two EVs (including a new Tesla), explicit Dominion rate-increase frustration, 10,000 kWh annual usage, and a forward-looking load increase already articulated. His one real concern is the slate roof: he wants ground mount options and is worried about structural damage. Lead with the ground mount conversation and have specifics ready. His wife is on the title and was not included in the booking — confirm she will be present before starting the proposal.
